mappings.rationaleAI: Directly covers the full arc of human evolution over six million years including Australopithecus, Homo habilis, Homo erectus, and Homo sapiens, the fossil record from East Africa, bipedalism, brain enlargement, and the Out of Africa hypothesis.

Covers palaeoanthropological evidence for early hominin evolution, key fossil discoveries in Africa, the evolutionary significance of bipedalism and tool manufacture, and the relationship between Homo sapiens and Neanderthals.

Directly covers Homo erectus including its emergence in Africa, dispersal across Asia and Europe, increased brain size, Acheulean hand axes, fire control, and debates about species boundaries -- precisely corresponding to outline path C.2.c.

Directly covers the Neanderthals including physical characteristics, tool-making, burial practices, adaptation to ice age climates, interbreeding with Homo sapiens, and extinction theories -- precisely corresponding to outline path C.2.d.ii.

Upper Paleolithic cave art (Lascaux, Chauvet, Altamira) was produced by Cro-Magnon (anatomically modern) humans, and the episode covers what the art reveals about cognitive development and symbolic thinking in early Homo sapiens.

The evolution of teeth covers human dental evolution and what fossil teeth reveal about evolutionary history -- directly relevant to morphological characteristics of Hominidae and the discovery of the hominid fossil record.

Alfred Russel Wallace's identification of the Wallace Line and his work on biogeography and evolution, including his divergence from Darwin on human evolution specifically, is relevant to the evolutionary process and primate paleontology.

Lamarck's pre-Darwinian evolutionary theory and the distinction between Lamarckism and natural selection, with modern reassessments through epigenetics, provide foundational context for understanding the evolutionary process.

Feathered dinosaurs establishing the link between theropod dinosaurs and birds illustrate the evolutionary paleontology of vertebrates and provide context for understanding the broader primate evolutionary tree.
